targets.name: Handa Manufacturing Pty Ltd as trustee for the Handa Manufacturing Trust

merger_description:
SHAPE Australia Corporation Limited (**SHAPE**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in Australian Professional Shopfitters Pty. Ltd (**APS**), pursuant to a share purchase agreement (the **Acquisition**). APS is currently wholly owned by Handa Manufacturing Pty Ltd as trustee for the Handa Manufacturing Trust.

SHAPE is an Australian publicly listed company (ASX: SHA). SHAPE provides a diverse range of construction services and products, including facilities maintenance, fitouts, and new builds, to customers in the office, transport, hotel, health, education, retail including retail fuel, government, aged care, community, data centres, defence, entertainment and recreation, industrial, and transport sectors in Australia.

APS provides commercial fitout services and products, including shopfitting, manufacturing, project management and transport services, to customers in the retail sector in Australia. APS also has a warehouse in Melbourne supplying custom joinery for fitouts, almost all of which is supplied in-house for its own fitout projects.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
SHAPE Australia Corporation Limited (SHAPE) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
the issued share capital in Australian Professional Shopfitters Pty.
Ltd (APS), as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, SHAPE, is an Australian publicly listed company (ASX:
SHA). SHAPE provides a diverse range of construction services and
products, including facilities maintenance, fitouts, and new builds, to
customers in the office, transport, hotel, health, education, retail
including retail fuel, government, aged care, community, data
centres, defence, entertainment and recreation, industrial, and
transport sectors in Australia.
The target, APS, provides commercial fitout services and products,
including shopfitting, manufacturing, project management and
transport services, to customers in the retail sector in Australia. APS
also has a warehouse in Melbourne supplying custom joinery for
fitouts, almost all of which is supplied in-house for its own fitout
projects.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between SHAPE Australia
and APS in the supply of commercial fitout services in the
retail sector, and the increase in SHAPE’s share of supply of
commercial fitout services to non-residential customers is
estimated to be small
b. there are alternative suppliers supplying commercial fitout
services and joinery to customers, including for the retail
sector, at both national and local level
c. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical
or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct, bundling or tying of services post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
